title: Saliency, Side by Side
tagline: Which pixels does your classifier actually use? Four attribution methods, one image
summary: On a synthetic 'thermal scene' with hidden ground truth, a hand-crafted classifier predicts the class. Vanilla gradient, SmoothGrad, Integrated Gradients and Grad-CAM compute saliency live and side-by-side. See where they agree, where they disagree, and the well-known reasons each one can lie.
highlights: Four saliency maps on the same image: vanilla gradient, SmoothGrad, IG, Grad-CAM.
Toggle the synthetic image (hot blob / two blobs / striped) and see the maps update.
Why each method can lie — concrete failure modes (saturation, noisy gradients, coarse localisation).
Lab-aligned: thermal-image attribution for ApneaEye / JoulesEye explanations.
